title: GitHub Review by sourcery-ai[bot]
summary: In `pushSecretRelevantChangePredicate.Update`, guard against a nil `DeletionTimestamp` before calling `IsZero()` (e.g., compare `GetDeletionTimestamp() == nil`) to avoid a potential nil pointer dereference.
external_feedback:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- In `pushSecretRelevantChangePredicate.Update`, calling `GetDeletionTimestamp().IsZero()` on `ObjectOld`/`ObjectNew` can dereference a nil pointer; it would be safer to guard against `nil` (e.g., compare `GetDeletionTimestamp() == nil` instead) before checking for presence changes.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- In `pushSecretRelevantChangePredicate.Update`, calling `GetDeletionTimestamp().IsZero()` on `ObjectOld`/`ObjectNew` can dereference a nil pointer; it would be safer to guard against `nil` (e.g., compare `GetDeletionTimestamp() == nil` instead) before checking for presence changes.
~~~

</details>

***

resolution_summary: Addressed the sole review comment by replacing `GetDeletionTimestamp().IsZero()` with an explicit `== nil` comparison on the *metav1.Time pointer in `pushSecretRelevantChangePredicate.Update` at operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:922, and added a defensive `if e.ObjectOld == nil || e.ObjectNew == nil { return true }` guard at the top of the UpdateFunc (line 916). A docblock above the predicate (lines 900-906) explains the rationale — `== nil` removes the implicit dependency on metav1.Time's nil-receiver guard and makes safety obvious to readers. No out-of-scope changes were made.
